output_freshness.analyst: ~0.3 hours ago (latest-analysis-summary.txt updated 10:23 UTC, last analysis JSON 2026-04-05T19:41)
output_freshness.curmudgeon: ~0.2 hours ago (WIN-038.c2.json at 10:29 UTC, tracker shows 38/67 Cycle 2 WINs reviewed)
output_freshness.decider: ~0.1 hours ago (daily-report-2026-04-08T06-30.json at 10:35 UTC, multiple runs today)

handoff_issues.description: RECURRING: digest-reviews.js processedWinIds fallback silently skips ALL new Cycle 2 reviews for WINs whose Cycle 1 review is already processed. 5 unprocessed c2 reviews (WIN-034 through WIN-038) exist on disk but digest shows 0 pending entries, and decider processed 0 reviews in its last 4 runs.
handoff_issues.evidence: 38 c2 review files on disk, 33 in processed-reviews.json (WIN-001.c2 through WIN-033.c2). Pending-digest.json generated at 09:23 shows 0 entries. Decider daily-report-2026-04-08T06-30.json has curmudgeon_reviews_processed: []. Root cause: build-scripts/digest-reviews.js line 143-144: `processedWinIds.has(entry.win_id)` matches 'WIN-034' from the Cycle 1 file 'WIN-034.json' already in the ledger, causing WIN-034.c2.json to be treated as processed despite being a new review.

suggested_fix.rationale: The processedWinIds fallback was added for legacy compatibility when the ledger contained bare IDs like 'WIN-034' instead of filenames. Now all ledger entries use filenames ('WIN-034.json', 'WIN-034.c2.json'). The fallback maps ALL processed filenames to bare WIN IDs, so any new cycle file for a previously-processed WIN is silently treated as already processed. This was flagged as P1 in the 2026-04-06T21-24 tinker report but remains unfixed. Removing the fallback to processedWinIds is safe because: (a) the processedSet already contains full filenames, and (b) any legacy bare-ID entries would be matched by processedSet.has(entry.win_id) on line 143 if needed — but we can simplify to filename-only since the ledger has been filename-based since the processed-reviews.json migration.

prompt_issues.description: Decider prompt at 413 lines — 65% over the 250-line ceiling. Previous tinker (P3) flagged at 339 lines; it has GROWN by 74 lines instead of shrinking. Long prompts cause LLMs to deprioritize earlier instructions, particularly the data flow rules in the first half.

suggested_fix.rationale: Not self-fixable. Recommend human refactor: move the patch template schema, severity definitions, and expansion integration procedure into separate reference files (e.g., monitor/prompts/decider-schemas.md, monitor/prompts/decider-expansion-proc.md) that the decider reads at runtime. Keep the main prompt under 250 lines focused on procedure and judgment rules.

prompt_issues.description: Analyst prompt at 296 lines — 18% over the 250-line ceiling. Lower priority than decider but trending upward.

suggested_fix.rationale: Not self-fixable. Recommend extracting Mode 3 (globe fingerprint hunt) procedure into a separate reference file since it's the lowest-priority mode and takes up the most space.

prompt_issues.description: Config.json schedule fields are stale: decider says 'daily 6:30 AM UTC' but runs multiple times daily (4+ runs today); curmudgeon says 'every 15 minutes' but CLAUDE.md says 10 minutes; analyst says 'every 8 hours' but CLAUDE.md says 30 minutes. Config is a reference doc, not functional, but stale values mislead tinker/human audits.

suggested_fix.find:
"curmudgeon": { "model": "opus", "schedule": "every 15 minutes", "role": "adversarial self-review of review arguments" },
    "decider": { "model": "opus", "schedule": "daily 6:30 AM UTC", "role": "triage actions, morning briefing, suggested patches" }
suggested_fix.replace:
"curmudgeon": { "model": "opus", "schedule": "every 10 minutes", "role": "adversarial self-review of review arguments" },
    "decider": { "model": "opus", "schedule": "every 20 minutes", "role": "triage actions, morning briefing, suggested patches" }
suggested_fix.rationale: Align config.json with actual scheduled task intervals per CLAUDE.md.

repeated_deferrals.description: SEC-3.1 Live Power page not archived. Status: 'assigned-analyst' but requires poller enhancement, not analyst work. Has been deferred across multiple decider runs.
repeated_deferrals.recommendation: Re-categorize as pending-human or infrastructure — the analyst cannot archive external pages.

schedule_issues.description: Decider runs significantly more often than config suggests (4-6 runs/day vs documented 'daily 6:30 AM'). This is working well — the frequent runs keep up with curmudgeon output. No action needed on schedule itself, just config accuracy (addressed in prompt_issues).

Globe fingerprint hunt (analyst Mode 3) has 0/34 items complete. The analyst is never idle enough to enter Mode 3 because Modes 1-2 always have work. This is expected behavior but means the fingerprint hunt will not progress until expansion backlog clears (6 pending EXP items).

recommendations.description: Fix digest-reviews.js line 143-144: remove processedWinIds fallback that silently blocks all new Cycle 2 reviews from reaching the decider. Replace the 3-way isProcessed check with `processedSet.has(file)` only. This is the same P1 from the 2026-04-06 tinker report — still unfixed and causing the entire curmudgeon→decider pipeline to stall for Cycle 2 items WIN-034+.
recommendations.rationale: Cannot self-fix because changes to build-scripts/ need a build cycle. Flagging for human application.

recommendations.description: Update monitor/config.json agent schedule fields to match actual intervals: curmudgeon=10min, decider=20min, analyst=30min.
Refactor decider.md (413 lines → target <250). Extract patch template schema, severity matrix, and expansion integration procedure into separate reference files.
ISS-074 (Live Power page archival) is classified as assigned-analyst but requires infrastructure work (poller enhancement or manual wget). Reclassify to pending-human or infrastructure.
Extract analyst Mode 3 (globe fingerprint hunt) procedure into a separate file to bring analyst.md under 250 lines.
